retry_reason: Failure means no mapping exists for that address; retrying immediately without changing state will also fail.
contract_summary: This function finds a panfrost_gem_mapping for a given virtual address and address space, and acquires a reference (via kref_get) on it. The caller receives a pointer to the mapping with an incremented refcount and must release it by calling the corresponding put function when done. If no mapping exists, returns NULL — the caller must check the return value before using it. The function holds spinlocks during the lookup but releases them before returning, so no lock is held by the caller.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to look up a panfrost_gem_mapping by address and increment its refcount via kref_get; the lookup logic is minimal and integral to locating the object to which the reference is acquired.

check.reason: kref_get(&mapping->refcount) is called on the mapping that is returned. The access path from the return value (mapping) to the refcount field is mapping->refcount (of type kref) and then kref->refcount (refcount_t), matching 'mapping.refcount.refcount'. The type chain panfrost_gem_mapping--kref--refcount_t is consistent. The operation is a get on the return value.
